name: AI Product Workflow
description: I treat AI as part of the product team, not just a place to write prompts. It participates in research capture, product definition, document chaining, and code translation.
scenarios: Turning raw user-demand samples into a clearer product direction quickly
Keeping MRD, BRD, PRD, and MVP scope inside one continuous context

name: Product Thinking
description: I define products around user value, transaction logic, scope boundaries, and deliberate subtraction instead of starting from a long feature list.
scenarios: Narrowing the problem space for vertical scenarios such as gym users
Turning fuzzy ideas into clear narratives around problem, user, and value

name: Content Modeling
description: I like to structure content into explicit fields before shaping the UI, so projects, books, and personal narrative can stay reusable and easy to extend.
scenarios: Designing maintainable schemas for about, projects, books, and skills
Turning personal-brand expression into a system instead of a one-off refresh

name: Next.js
description: When I need to balance content pages, SEO, product storytelling, and future expansion, Next.js is the delivery layer I trust most.
scenarios: Shipping personal sites that combine a content system with product narrative
Preparing a scalable frontend surface for a product still in active iteration

name: React
description: I use React to keep component structure and state boundaries clear so the site can grow in content without turning harder to maintain.
scenarios: Building reusable cards and sections for profile, project, and reading pages
Leaving room for future product interaction without breaking the page system

name: Prototype Translation
description: I am actively practicing a shorter path from design intent to working UI by translating markdown and HTML prototypes more directly into code.
scenarios: Handing structured markdown to AI and turning it into pages or components
Using HTML files as a fast visual checkpoint before full implementation
